channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. — 80 replies — 79.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in.

Open thread • Replies: 80 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:07 UTC

OP

I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. I’m just completely alone in my apartment with nothing important to do, lonely. I might as well not exist at all. It’s like i’m the biggest loser in high school or something. The people here who advocate for the NEET lifestyle must be some real freaks.

Early replies (first 3)

62201436
I am truly sorry to hear that you dont have anything meaningful to do in your life or have an identity besides being a wageslave. This is entirely a (you) problem

62201447
Apartment? Let me guess youre living in some shitty city surrounded by brown people in some shitty apartment and youre wondering why your life is shit. You need to fix your environment, you do not need to hypnotize yourself by working some bullshit job that you dont care about

62201460
>>62201428
I'm sorry to say that you've been brainwashed. If you can't do anything but work then you should kill yourself. Actually don't because we need people like you to do "important things" like putting our fries in the bag

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62203008
>>62201969
>Oh noooo men can’t force with violence women into breeding with men they don’t find attractive anymore!
only low T men think this is a good thing

62203201
>>62203008
High T men don't even have to try to get women to sleep with them. Low T, ugly, poor, beta faggots are the ones that need to force women, kek.

62203328
It depends on your personality and other factors. For me I'm able to handle it usually but now I'm sick and it prevents me from doing the activities I liked to do as a neet. Feels like I'm waiting to die but it was relatively fun while it lasted. Too bad money couldn't save me in the end

62203501
>>62202019
Oh no its the insufferable doctor again that likely to discuss endlessly and is high on his intelligence but a typical midwit as most doctors.
>>62201969
>Oh noooo men can’t force with violence women into breeding with men they don’t find attractive anymore! Only low value men care about that. Do only low value men like neeting?
Classical strawman. Its not about forcing to breend. Its about changing incentives back to normal without propaganda and support from the government. Also its all about your face, not your "value". You would understand if you were not handsome.
>>62202041
Bullshit. These are 3 of 1000s of historical systems. And even these, britain was 100% based on race. rome too (barbarians) and the persians had words for "non aryans". So literally 100% wrong.
>>62201966
Vielleicht helfen die neuen Maßnahmen, mehr Geld für Rentner und Beamte und höhere Steuern. XD
Aber im Ernst es wird nur ein langsamer Abstieg. Zusammenbruch gibts nur bei Krieg oder sowas.

62203529
>>62203501
>Also its all about your face, not your "value". You would understand if you were not handsome.
Wrong. I should have said mostly, or its the basis. Of course status and money and character matter.

62203638
>>62201588
You kind of sound like a faggot

62204797
>>62203501
There were a lot of romaniced barbarians in the romam empire and they could work in high positions in rome. There are huge amount of multicultural empires in history and once again we’re all results of different populstions mixing. The german idea of a pure race is another too simple and short sighted ideology from back when people didn’t know just how much we are results of different populations mixing over time. Even germany isn’t one race but had celtic mixture in the south and slavic on the east. Britain was celtic and three different kinds of germanic. I’m not saying go full blanda upp but migration and multiculturalism has always existed. Sure you we can try to make the flow lesser but completely ending it is a pipe dream. And why should we? There are a lot of good immigrants. All in all it’s no excuse to think ”society is shit” unless you got mind broken by /pol/-propaganda.

62204835
>>62201428
Ahh goycattle Stockholm Syndrome. Have you read about the freed slaves after the Civil War who killed themselves?

62204853
The conclusion I have come to is that 99% of /biz/ is depressed and/or facing existential crisis.

62204867
>>62201428
The bad news: You are a brainless golem that can't do anything unless he's commanded to by someone else.
The good news: You're in good company, since this is most of humanity.

Now just go get another wagejob, wagegolem.

62205336
>>62204853
Sounds like it. Would be nice to have this discussion brought over to /lit/.

62205354
>>62201588
> without any reason for it
This is such short-term thinking. You may have a problem with instant gratification.
What’d you lose your job for and why are you shitposting about NEETs inserts of asking for advice on getting a job?

62205600
>>62201428
So this is what a natural slave feels like when he stop working. Thanks for the information.

62205607
>>62201428
>I’m just completely alone in my apartment with nothing important to do, lonely.
Start with the greeks.

62205631
>>62201588
>Yeah these are nice but ultimately i still feel fucking lonely
Ok i get it you are an extraverted type. That figures. Do you know that this board is one of the most introverted people place on the whole internet?
We introverts, especially INTJs, which is overepresented here, very much dislike social interaction, except perhaps with a few trusted friends. We don't really feel lonely ever, and even if we do sometimes, we prefer to feel lonely that being with normies.

62205702
>>62201588
>Women have a lot more sex with you when you work, especially if it’s a high status, high paying job.
This is the only reason i could envy high status wageslaves. But you have to be pretty up far in the hierarchy in a big company to have access to multiple pretty women wanting to spend "time" with you. Like a board member or a branch director in S&P 500 company. If you are a CEO of a small 50 people company, you'll have have a few MILF or young average and that's it. Something that can be done as a NEET.

62205726
>>62201428
>I love being a goodgoy wageslave
>the only value i have in life is what i provide to mr shekelberg
pathetic

62205752
>>62201436
/thread

62205758
>>62201612
>All in all this just feels fucking lonely, like how a very unpopular loser kid would live.
Boy WE were the losers. Not really because we didn't have any choice, but because we hated it, this society, school, from the start. We could have played the game, work at school, play the social hierarchy game, but it's something we depise. And don't get us wrong, not all, but many of us NEET introvers, will get way further in life than you ever will, doing what we do. The others who fail, will have a nice NEET life, free of hassle, or have a solitary job. You do you, we do us.

62205785
>>62202019
>Why do you want to assume my job? I’m a doctor.
That figure you allopathic scum. Disappointed i wouldn't have had a discussion with you if i knew this from the start as you are not even a real human.

62205905
>>62202878
Is it ever tiresome having to deal with self-inflicted conditions? Did you change your lifestyle from seeing what happens to others?

62206218
I feel ya, OP. I quit my job recently because it just sucked too much to keep doing it and I'd saved up a good bit of money, but it is rough how you have no structure throughout the day. If I was younger, I'd probably love it, but at a certain age, you've become familiar with most of life and it's hard to find novelty anymore. It's also hard to find ways to be helpful to others on your own.

What I'm doing though is working everyday on a project of my own. It's not easy, and I get frustrated every day, but progress is inevitable if you show up every day and put in the time. It's either work for yourself or work for someone else, both are difficult in their own ways, but you get to choose. I'd say make sure you try both before you decide how to spend your life.

62206367
>>62205600
>>62205607
>>62205631
>>62205702
>>62205758
>>62205785

Please stop posting. Nobody values your input/opinions.

62206531
>>62201428
If you're that desperate for a job just go do free volunteer work to put on your resume
>Inb4 the whole circular argument of "oh but that doesn't pay bills" into "I thought you wanted a job to have purpose tho" into "well I need to be making progress in life" normie bullshit

62206541
>>62202019
I do have animals. The point is that I get to choose how much I work. I could have more animals and do a lot more work but I choose not to. I could have employees but when I was an employee I decided that I would never want to become a boss and I would only work for myself. I used to be a welder for a motorhome company and after the great recession they were cutting hours for people and I decided I would just work 4 hours every day so some people could keep their hours and I realized 4 hours per day was perfect because I got the warm feeling of satisfaction without getting burnt out and tired.

Anyway my banker keeps wanting me to expand and my portfolio is worth almost as much as my dad's farm. I just don't want all the extra hassle I guess. I already grow enough food for 1,000 people and I think that should be enough for 1 person to do. I just want to work my 9 to noon and then cook lunch and play a round of golf.

62206649
>>62201428
you're not supposed to neet in some boring studio apartment
you're supposed to neet in your boomer parents' huge ass house

62206681
>>62205905
>Did you change your lifestyle from seeing what happens to others?

Yeah it has made me drink less and lose weight.

>Is it ever tiresome having to deal with self-inflicted conditions?

Majority of things that require treatment are partially self-inflicted. I don’t really judge because i don’t believe that people should live only for health.

62206763
>>62205785
Funny how much medicine always awakens emotion in people.

62207574
>>62204797
>but migration and multiculturalism has always existed.
Why do you repeat jewish lines. Migration from nonwhites to europe never existed. And Rome was maybe discutable, all your other "countless example" are not existing.

62210030
>>62201543
If you can’t find any meaningful things to do with your life that doesnt involve working for a paycheck, you are an empty husk of a human. Just another pre-programmed worker bee

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 70 replies — 78.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:14 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62207560
>>62207486
it's the product that will matter in the end and the users choice

62207604
>>62207106
Wake up. 100x is no longer exists

62207815
>>62207560
True. End of the day users decide. Doubt a16zcrypto and GoMining would bother getting involved if they saw no potential there.

62207855
Buy ONDO and HBAR

62207863
>>62207855
your targets?

62207902
>>62195293
Why would you buy now, then sell near previous ATH?

62207947
>>62207815
Is there such a thing like this. All I see is the defi hacks and exploits. Came to realize it might be another inside job and devs milking

62207965
>>62207902
I think Bitcoin gonna do at least x3 this cycle

62207996
>>62201349
What's the problem with that? Some people hold gems for a reason

62208030
>>62207947
Guess we’ll find out once TBVs are live since that’s when the real use cases start unlocking too. Can’t even trust random protocols anymore after all the exploit drama every cycle.

62208072
I think the days of everything pumping together are over.

62208095
>>62207902
If you can’t handle volatility, just stick with BTC.

62208114
>>62195275
BTC dominance still killing most alt momentum rn.

62208155
>>62195275
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Wth

62208168
>>62208095
I was asking why he would sell at 120k....

If it reaches ATH again, it's going to go higher.

62209247
>>62205030
Good choice anon. Never really liked the whole wrapped BTC meta either.

62209253
>>62208030
Yeah and still think the space matures eventually once better infrastructure and security become the priority.

62209260
>>62208168
Are you also holding?

62209263
>>62207947
Yeah true, but can’t really generalize everything either. Some protocols got hacked for completely different reasons too.

62209287
>>62208072
days over? You're lying

62209291
>>62209260
Hodl

62209304
>>62195275
BTC dominance still looks too strong for a full alt run right now.

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

item.title: >It doesn't matter what the average or median income is, it's all about where you live & how smart you are with money. — 24 replies — 42.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>It doesn't matter what the average or median income is, it's all about where you live & how smart you are with money.

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:34 UTC

OP

>It doesn't matter what the average or median income is, it's all about where you live & how smart you are with money. If you make 65k in LA, NYC, Chicago, etc. you're broke as fuck while someone making 35k in a small midwestern town is living comfortably. You also wouldn't have to deal with drugs, crime, diversity, homeless, etc. So yeah, I'll take option 2 all day.

Why do rural suburban losers always say this? It is like saying you couldn’t make it in a nice area and now you have to move to a crappier area.

If that were true, why do wealthy people concentrate together in certain areas?

Early replies (first 3)

62208127
successful people get jobs in rich areas, I heard.
even crazier, those jobs pay well. I know right?

62208139
>>62208127
What are you saying?
We are talking about the guy who made the quote and whether his opinion is retarded or not.

62208178
>>62208139
nobody who lives in LA, NYC, or Chicago makes $65K, everyone who went to college makes at least $110-$150K unless that quote was from 1980 or something. this thread is gay. sage.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208182
>>62208178
Are you stupid?
A ton of people living in those places can barely afford rent.

62208378
>>62208100
>why do wealthy people concentrate together in certain areas?
Greater fool theory

62208379
>>62208182
That doesn't change the fact people make over 6 figures in major cities with degrees

62208418
>>62208379
Not everyone. Maybe less than half. Big cities are playgrounds for the rich and everyone else is there to serve them.

62208482
>>62208379
You don't live in the real world and it shows.

62208508
>>62208100
The trick is to make enough money in a high cost of living area that you can move to a low cost of living area (not to be confused with a poor area) and live a better life. Work for a decade in a city to wagemaxx, live as far below your means as you can, and investmaxx so you don’t have to wage forever. For 99% of people this is the way out.

62208512
>>62208100
>rural suburban losers

62208595
>>62208508
>i am a loser who couldn’t hang with winners so I move into loser area
Yeah, no

62208644
>>62208508
This is Meta strategy I plan on following.

>Live in NYC area. Right now, w/ parents. Stacking cash & investing.
>Looking to retire in less than 15 years (around 35-40).
>Move to beautiful low-cost area. Maybe Idaho. Maybe Japan.

62208687
>>62208595
Sure sure, the places filled with big homes and families living what gets constantly shilled online as an unobtainable dream... THAT'S the place for losers.

62208731
>>62208687
What?
The unobtainable dream is a house in the city but with lots of space and away from noise and riff raff.

62208735
>>62208687
What the fuck are you talking about?
People that cannot afford good areas move away from cities. The farther away the cheaper the properties.

62208751
>>62208100
>looks like a motivational thread
>peek inside
>demotivational

I'm here to tell you all, you can make it. You can be happy. You can retire early. You will find love. You will hang in there.

As long as you're not Indian lol theyre fucked forever.

62208846
>>62208751
Unfortunately, the average person is falling behind.

62208878
>>62208735
Those cheap properties far from cities have some areas with pretty damn big houses surrounded by pretty damn good nature and pretty damn awesome communities. But you'd rather be a pet for the 1% than consider improving your life because you've internalized some meme that your pod city life is high status.

Nice samefagging btw. If the average person is falling behind consider what dumb herd behaviors they're falling into and then break yourself free from it.

62209009
>>62208100
>You also wouldn't have to deal with drugs, crime, diversity, homeless, etc.
lol

62209014
>>62208878
High status is “pretty damn big houses surrounded by pretty damn good nature and pretty damn awesome communities” INSIDE the city. You fucking ruralfag.

62209025
> Those cheap properties far from cities have some areas with pretty damn big houses surrounded by pretty damn good nature and pretty damn awesome communities.
Location location location.

62209026
>>62208878
> If the average person is falling behind consider what dumb herd behaviors they're falling into and then break yourself free from it.
Why are you getting personal with me? I live in one of the areas I am describing. lol.

62209898
>>62208100
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62210093
>>62208508
This is it for me...and I'm taking it one step further by building my own house when I quit working. I will do 100% of the work myself. I am qualified, and taking 2 years and 200k or even less of my own money to build a non piece of shit house sounds better than taking a 500k mortgage on a money pit I additionally have to insure. Sand, rocks, and cement are cheaper than lumber if you do it yourself. My copper roof will scrap for more than I paid for it adjusted for inflation in 100 years when my great great grandchildren finally have to do the first roof maintenance.

item.title: Day 1711 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 11 replies — 24.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1711 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:40 UTC

OP

Day 1711 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Early replies (first 3)

62200304
What's your prediction on 1m per btc? I'll say 2032.

62200344
>>62200304
2032 is the absolute earliest date. Between 2032 and 2036

62200413
>>62200296
you forgot your trip
>>62200304
it will take till the 2040s realistically

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200765
>>62200296
vely cute snale :D

62201283
>>62200296

62202756
snal

62202915
>>62200296
TAKE MY ENERGY

62207623
snoooool

62207786
>>62200296
fake count fuckery you should rope yourself and take your shit coin with you

62208653
>>62200296
cute

62210116
snhale

62199219

snol

Day 1712 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

fake fraudulent fuck stop wasting our time and neck yourself u cunt

snale :D

62208904

cute

item.title: KABOOOOOM! **BREAKING** RIPPLE XRP NEWS — 4 replies — 11.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
KABOOOOOM! **BREAKING** RIPPLE XRP NEWS

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:46 UTC

OP

This is **MASSIVE** for Ripple XRP. A top analyst in the cryptocurrency space predicts the XRP price reaching a value of $3million per coin.

You Are Not Bullish Enough!

Early replies (first 3)

62210113
>>62210043
BOOOOM

62210129
>>62210043
maybe provide a link, fag

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62210137
gay and retarded threads like this are why I didn't go all in on sandisk calls in january. I would have 360,000 dollars.

item.title: no one cares about SWE — 4 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
no one cares about SWE

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:32 UTC

OP

seriously who cares about tech guys like software engineers or code monkeys now?

The real skill now is in sales, marketing, and business sense. No one cares about ur app or product if it cant get its first 100 users. You can pay some indian or jungle asian to code anything you need. Maybe the real skill has always been in marketing and sales/business and never coding

Early replies (first 3)

62208926
Its always been about connections

Back in the early-mid 2010's there was literally a guy you had to pay (insider) to get your app on the apple store like 6 figures minimum and he'd help you get in

I know because I worked for a nepo baby who ran a company (funded by his billionaire family) and we made lots of apps and paid the insider fee to get listed and pushed on the app instantly

This game has never been fair and never will be fair

62208929
>>62208926
oh and yeah the nepo baby did make 9 figures himself because of this

all from his family funding his company to pay these kinds of insider/middle-men to get priority and pushed the app store

whats funny is the media has made the nepo baby out to be like some kind of self-made success story rags to riches, when his family (parents and grandparents) are literally multi-billionaires

he is also literally jewish too kek

62208971
>Maybe the real skill has always been in marketing and sales/business and never coding

the real skills that pay well will continue to be immaterial and tangentially or directly related to finance. like sales,business, law and cryptocurrency

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5WPB2u8EzL8&t=2757s

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62210091
>>62208893
That's completely right and it's even more apparent with AI.

The real value of small app is the marketing - anyone can build anything small in a few weeks.

But at larger scale, think say Google search, you can't just copy it, it takes huge datasets, huge capital and large talented teams to get anywhere near something viable, in this scenario a good software engineer is worth it

item.title: Day 296 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100 — 4 replies — 3.8/hr

Day 296 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

62209718

62209721
>>62209714

62209763
>>62209714

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62210022
>>62209763
Yeah, I've never clicked on it either. Yeah.

item.title: thank you intel and thank you trump! — 2 replies — 2.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
thank you intel and thank you trump!

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:25 UTC

OP

>saves the US economy
people need to read a fucking chart, there is no fucking recession. Intel is built for AI (they say it on their website), all AI relies on intel and nvidia does nothing. Intel will lead the AI RACE thats why they are worth $126 now. Target valuation should be $250 (conservative estimate). But realistically I think we will hit $450 within a few months. However if thing go good we could see hte price go to $700 and then I would imagine it would crab there for a bit.

Early replies (first 3)

62208502
Intel's a fucking distraction

62210074
If intc is gonnabe 700, tsm will be 6900 unless Trump nukes taiwan

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML? — 42 replies — 35.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML?

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-05-09 15:55 UTC

OP

it has vastly overtaken monero. but it is not "used" as much as monero

Early replies (first 3)

62194143
>>62193936
>it has vastly overtaken monero.
The last time it has overtaken XMR twitter personalities got paid to shill zec.
>therefore being able to skim through AML?
Depends on how the law is written, definitely a grey area for many AML laws.

62194158
>>62193936
Monero is for midwits

62194187
>>62194158
>Monero is for midwits
true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200065
>>62195139
what desktop infra do you need? ywallet works on my machine

62200161
Truly decentralized CPU only privacy coins are gonna be huge. Grab a bag before they take off.

62200222
No one uses zcash

Monero is the most used crypto on darknet markets. Its the only crypto with product market fit besides bitcoin.

62200292
>>62193936
Optional privacy means any use of privacy will automatically be seen as suspicious.

62200958
>>62193936
zcash is better for speculators
monero is more useful. it's like comparing BTC to ETH

62201103
>>62200958
>zcash is better for speculators
How do you speculate on something with piss-poor adoption prospects?

62201118
>>62200958
>monero is more useful
False. Zcash does Bitcoin and Monero's jobs better than both of them.

62201192
>>62201103
Bigger fool

62203122
The privacy landscape today and its problems:

1) Monero RingCT: the issue: weak privacy, extremely easy to filter out decoys from real spends. The good: proofs verified by your node, low inflation risk

2) Monero FCMP: the issue: optional privacy, weak due to full view keys that can be aggregated to monitor flows in & out of respective wallets. Or they can be flat out leaked through non reproducible wallet implementation backdoors and become a master key that completely deanonymizes the network. Security: high inflation risk because you don't verify transaction proofs at your node, it uses a zero knowledge proof circuit ("FCMP++") and you only verify what the circuit says about the transaction.

3) Zcash: the issue: optional privacy. Mitigation possible by controlling the timing and amounts of the unshielding process. Full view keys of shielded pool wallets could be leaked through non reproducible binaries. Security: high inflation risk because again you cannot verify transaction proofs directly with your node, it uses a zero knowledge proof circuit ("SNARKs") and your node only verified what the SNARK says.

4) Dero: the issue: Captain minted 2M coins for himself in the first year of deployment (2022) when binaries were not reproducible. That's 9% of the supply (9/23). The good: strongest privacy by default, no view keys (nothing to leak), low inflation risk because it uses homomorphic encryption, all transactions are verified directly by your node homomorphically xyou don't trust a binary circuit.

Now pick your poison.

I'd mention other lowcaps but the real choice is between Xmr, Zec and Dero because other privacy coins follow either the Xmr model or the Zec model. For example Salvium is a Monero fork that already uses CARROT for "compliance". ARRR uses Zec's SNARKs but is run by a team that just copies Zec's updates without even understanding the inherent inflation risk. If Arrr is/was ever exploited there will be no way to know, no red flags.

62203128
>>62203122
Grin is similar to Monero but without rings and stealth addresses, it uses UTXO with Pedersen Commitments but since it has no rings no nullifiers are needed because there is no sender privacy (no decoys). Beam similar to Grin, no sender privacy so again very easy to build the transaction graph.

Dero introduces a new standard by using a different primitive (El Gamal) and no utxo model (the only one to do so) which is the strongest option tech wise because it makes it impossible to build a transaction graph.

Xelis stands to Dero like Grin to Monero, it uses Ristretto ElGamal which means no sender/receiver privacy because it's not public key rerandomizable so it's not possible to have decoys. You can tell the exact sender and receiver of each transaction just by looking it up in an explorer. But since it uses HE also in Xelis it's not possible, or at least much harder compared to Cryptonote coins, to build a transaction graph. Still it's more transparent than Dero since you can study the full transaction history of each account and look for behavioral patterns. Ie: if you use Xelis an outsider will know exactly which other accounts/exchanges you sent money to and received money from even though they don't see amounts.

Dero is the only coin with privacy by default, and the most advanced implementation of El Gamal, where you can't build a transaction graph while being able to verify all TX proofs w/ your node directly.

But can you live with the fact that the Captains behind it minted 2.2M coins for themselves after spending the first pre-mine in the 2021 bull? That's the question. That's pure pathological greed on Captain's side. Or, more realistically, "Captains" since Captain is probably a fictitious identity used by devs that contribute to Dero under other pseudonymns to this day.

62203136
>>62203128
OTOH they released the best privacy tech to date by far. Maybe 2.2M is acceptable considering they can't ask for VC funding, have to lay low/stay anonymous and fund themselves somehow? Idk.

What I know is that if I'm looking for privacy, nothing else comes remotely close to Dero tech wise.

62203225
>>62203122
>>62203128
Midwit takes.
Real solution: coinjoin. On Bitcoin.
Also, LN, on Bitcoin.

Sorry, your alt is not needed.

62203313
>>62203225
Sorry, already been evaluated and rejected for sucking.

62203334
>>62203313
>some druggie is afraid of using tech
cool story bro

62203382
>>62203225
>coinjoin and ln
kek le bitshitter cope gtfo nigger

62203402
>>62195101
Druggies are midwits at best. Losers pissing their lives away over plants soaked in acetone.

62203465
>>62203334
>>some druggie is afraid of using tech
More like tech-savvy criminals with everything on the line have found BTC's privacy "solutions" inadequate and unreliable while Monero ticks all the required boxes.

62203635
>>62203465
You would get laughed at so much if you didn't take yourself so seriously. Try leaning into it instead of sneeding over criticism Mr ex XMR general OP

62203740
>>62203635
Non-rebuttal rebuttal.

62203823
>>62203122
sorry what? ZKPs do not cause "inflation risk"

62203853
>>62203740
You've been posting the same shitty takes for the better part of a decade, immune to all arguments. You don't deserve rebuttals at this point, you deserve ridicule.

62203902
>>62203853
Nice try but still a non-rebuttal rebuttal. Because you have no rebuttal. So you try to distract with ad hominems. Keep going.

62204119
>>62203122
>>62203128
>>62203136
>Dero shill

See >>62204099

62204445
>>62203902
My only thing I am arguing is that you're retarded and immune to logic. I reference your long history of being retarded and immune to logic, so that I don't have to slog through another retarded conversation with you. If that's an ad hominem, that's the point. I'm arguing about you, not Monero. Maybe you have trouble distinguishing between the two because it makes up 100% of your personality.

62204821
>>62203402
yes
but the liquidity on DNM is still dependent on those midwits
zcash shielded pool needs a specific wallet with anti-retard infra if it wants to even replace bitcoin on DNMs

62204824
>>62203334
that's about right
state channels have very garbage infra for self custody, most solutions involve routing through custodial as backup
that's too many footguns for DNM guys, you will never see LN adoption on any escrow, save for rare exceptions where two ultra-bitcoin nerds collide

62208887
zcash won. shit isn't going down.

62209857
>>62203122
>Or they can be flat out leaked through non reproducible wallet implementation backdoors and become a master key that completely deanonymizes the network
Regular wallet keys can also be leaked to deanonymize the network you lying fag.

What a load a FUD.

62209871
>>62193936
They are both not used as much as Litecoin with mweb

62209999
>>62209871
deluded

item.title: ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT… — 65 replies — 33.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT…

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-05-09 15:12 UTC

OP

ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT
STOP SELLING
HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER
40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT BEFORE SPREADING FUCK
I LITERALLY JUST BOUGHT BACK

Early replies (first 3)

62202000
Fuck off retard

62202033
>>62201978
>“it is possible more cases will be reported” because it can be up to six weeks before those infected with the Andes variant of hantavirus show symptoms.

Lmao 40% mortality with SIX WEEKS to transmit it would fucking DESTROY humanity if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.

62202089
>>62202033
Bullish on Hanta? Truly a nothing burger though, this disease is likely less transmissible than herpes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62204459
>>62201978
Do you think a second lockdown scenario would be highly bullish for crypto like the last one was?

62204460
>>62204459
Yes.

Here's the play:
>OTM puts the moment you see second degree transmission (patient zero transmitted to person who transmitted to someone else)
>SELL as SOON as the President is about to speak
>All into crypto (BTC/XMR)

62204470
>>62204346
This isn't like Covid in one very important way - we have known about Hantavirus for a half century now (it was discovered during the Korean war), and we know about this specific south american strain of hanta.
The transmission window is extremely short, less than a day, and it's blatantly obvious when you're potentially infectious because you're running an extremely high fever. Also, even during that period of peak infectiousness it's still not terribly transmissible, most hanta virus outbreaks involve just a few people, and most of those turn out to have gotten it directly from rodent droppings. The outbreak in arizona back in the 90's for example came from infected rodents in a warehouse that stored canned sodas, and the dust from their droppings was getting onto the top of the cans and people were getting infected from drinking the soda. It looked like person-to-person transmission within families until they figured out it was coming from the soda cans and everybody in the family was drinking the same sodas.

62204475
>>62202000

Fpbp

62204543
>>62201978
>Load ze Hantavirus FUD

62204585
>>62201978
The virus has between a 2 week and 8 week incubation time.

It spreads by aerosol droplets.

The virus can remain virulent within a droplet for between 2 and 15 days.

It has a 40% rate of mortality.

This is not 'we treat you with a vaccine' shit. This is 'we shoot you and burn the body' shit.

You won't hear about the billionaires running into their bunkers. They'll be there long before things get hairy.

62204672
>>62204585
Nah. This is another hoax to get everyone to inject another mark of the beast into their arm. Fuck that. So fucking obvious and they aint fooling anybody after the 1st round.

62204681
>>62204672
Get the jab, schizo! You are killing people!!!

62204692
>>62204681
Fuck off. I took the covid vax and im not taking another

62204874
Whats with the nonstop pandemics? The last one was just 6 years ago. Why we getting literal viral plague now?

62204956
>>62204672
there's no rule that they have to do the same thing twice. It could be different.

62204967
>>62204874
Israel's pr department are desperate to change the topic

62204988
>>62204967
I suppose this is it. Another nothing burger vax forced upon me

62205070
>>62202033
>if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.
It's not transmissible while asymptomatic, it's extremely hard to catch it while symptomatic (onset is like the flu so it can happen, but requires a lot of fluids and isn't aerosolized).

If you actually want to doomsay then the real issues is if it gets into rodent populations then it's just a recurring issue.

62205228
>>62205070
Then kill the rodents whats the big deal?

62205278
Stop falling for /pol/ bot threads. No one else cares about it at all

62205340
>>62202909
So like a one week timeframe they changed to possible human transmission? Just like this thing lol

62205341
>>62205278
Good point desu

62205352
humanity exists for 300000 years and now all of a sudden we have global humanity ending pandemics back to back within just a few years. yeah this seems a LITTLE bit unlikely and no I wont stop noticing.

62205358
>>62205340
Correct, they only have so much information so "the story" will change the moment they get more.

There's nothing wrong with that besides being a retard who thinks that since something is "the facts" all other possibilities have been "deboonked".

If you have an IQ over 110 you should be able to see the situation as one of many possibilities of varying probabilities, and update your weightings as more information becomes available.

Again, highly likely it won't transmit well enough but keep your eyes peeled for new cases.

62206237
>>62204470
We've known about corona viruses for a long ass time too. There's only been so many cases of this strain ever documented

62206252
>>62205352
Covid and now this banter plague would barely register in history; big nothingburgers. Mass media and the feverish obsession with keeping boomers alive as long as possible is what made covid register as a black-death tier plague in our (well, just normies') minds.

62206383
>>62206252
Just looked up the numbers again to rage-bait myself:

>According to provisional CDC mortality data, COVID‑19 death rates per 100,000 population rise steeply with age, from under1 in young children to over 1,395 per100,000 in those aged 85+ in 2021.

Imagine you're in Europe or China in 300ad, or the middle ages, and suddenly some unknown fever goes around. Out of everyone that contracts this mysterious fever, not even 0.1% dies. Literally NO ONE would care, and rightly so. The Justinian plague had a survival rate of less than 50%, now that is a pandemic to write home about.

62206439
>>62202089
It's a nothing burger to prepare you for a somethingburger

How would humanity react if they found out about sars? Mpox? Ebola?

Write down the findings and data and then plan your depopulation event accordingly.

One thing I've noticed on goytok is that normies are showing high levels of apathy and vitriol towards the current hanta passengers. This is likely to prepare them mentally to accept the coming deaths of the future.

tl:dr when cows are led to the slaughter they might freak out and go crazy. Imagine leading billions of cows and you're in a time crunch.

62206650
>>62206383
I wish it was the boomer destroyer they hyped it up to be.

62206967
>>62206237
And this is one of the documented and well-studied strains of hanta. Do try to keep up.

62208344
>>62202144
calls or puts

62208733
>>62202144
Unironically how do you make money from this?

62209911
>>62202190
Bill gates has modified it, made it easier to spread

item.title: Crypto is a literal meme — 55 replies — 24.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto is a literal meme

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-05-09 14:55 UTC

OP

Let’s be honest, crypto is already a meme and BTC is no exception.
But the real joke is Europe. In the EURSS, you can’t even buy BTC with euros without getting dragged through KYC, and cashing out is just as bad.
I’m on Bisq 2 because Bisq 1 got rekted, and the cheapest offer I can find for buys with SEPA has a 6% premium with a max buy of 85 EUR. 85 fucking euros. For Bitcoin. In 2026.
And selling back to euros? Absolute clown show, impossible to cash out without giving back to the nanny state at least 36% profits. What the hell is this shit system supposed to be?
I CANNOT BUY NOR SELL BITCOIN IN 2026 WITHOUT GETTING LAGARDE'S FIST UP MY ASSHOLE.

Early replies (first 3)

62209483
> still talks about Europe

62209504
>>62209475
OK so what can a jewropooran really do if he makes a good profit on crypto? Fly out to some Asian backwater, set up accounts there and sell? It would be good to know in the unlikely case that I make it because I hate jews too much to give them any of my hypothetical profits.

62209510
>>62209504
This is why I'm trapped. I cannot cash out. The Asian escape gateways require fiscal residence afaik. And I'm not moving to the yellow countries any time soon.
I don't wanna go suck arab dick either which also require for me to be a fiscal resident there (0% crypto taxes but can be nuked by the kikes or Iran anytime now)
South America same, but I'll get monkey aids instead (thinking Paraguay).
But I'm not giving lagarde my hard worked cash to continue funding infinite muslims and niggers, rather die and shove my trezor up my ass. Meanwhile I cannot accumulate because that's already a tax liability and I cannot sell.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62209683
>>62209676
>Why do you think that *thing irrelevant to Euro issue with a tax*
How about you stop posting like a bot and gain some actual contextual awareness?

62209688
>>62209661
Yup that's what I'll do invest in us markets and be done with it. At least I'm not considered a literal criminal.
>>62209672
Yup seems the 1 year tax free holding period is getting cut afaik

62209690
>>62209672
Germany is defacto bankrupt. Same goes for the entire rest of Europe. The only credit expansion Europe is able to generate is uncovered and public communist one. Europe and especially Germany has been in a deflationary death spirap for the better part of a decade. And every attempt, be it the import of debt collateral from the middle east, hyper inflationary monetary policy during the coof distraction, the loans to Hoholistan, the "Doppelwumms" all fizzled out and non managed to reverse Germany and Europe going ussr 1989 this year

62209697
>>62209690
At least now you're slightly more coherent with your bullshit, you got any receipts on those 3 million gorillions or is it all a larp?

62209703
>>62209697
Do you want the account numbers and the shell companies under which that capital is hidden as well? Shall I write you a fucking check. Stupid nigger!

62209707
>>62209703
Hahaha bro don't get upset i also was an OG with 8 figure sells and been in BTC since 25 years ago.
You probably don't even know what's anchor protocol nigger

62209709
>>62209649
you just have to do it slowly, or just 'buy a house' in the said country, but it seems like you are not really committed

62209712
>>62209707
No I have no idea what the scam you try to advertise is. Why should I? Crapto is over since it was integrated into the bankrupt boomer ponziconomy, its now just what it originally was, a wonky ass distributed database management system with no use case that isn't used for anything

62209715
>>62209709
I have already got a mortgage as primary residence. There's no point in paying the debt since I get some interest deduction and can make more via investments. It's just that everything is already optimized but I cannot escape and create more wealth. I feel stuck as a wagecuck forever

62209725
>>62209688
>At least I'm not considered a literal criminal.
for now. one of the big ideas in brussel right now is to (force) investments into the eu. stuff like that often done through tax benefits. at least thats how it starts, the next part is tax disadvantage for investments outside the us. its pretty much a laid out plan. the question is when is it fully rolled out, no one knows.

62209726
>>62209712
Alright. Anchor protocol was the UST minter machine for the terra Luna fiasco. If you were an OG you should at least know about terra and anchor hahahaha. Let's not even get into defi summer. Waiting on those 6-7 figure receipt and your panama offshore shell company btw

62209729
>>62209726
>Terra/Luna
>OG
Fucking hell, you stupid retarded zoomer trash. Sometimes I get the impression you are as braindead as the boomer trash

62209731
>>62209729
Still waiting on your receipts OG

62209737
>>62209715
no i'm saying if you want to cash it all out, you have to give the bank an excuse, but this plan requires you to abandon europe which it seems you are not committed to, cash everything out, getting a visa will be easy because you are tying your investment in said country, then use local bank card for whatever, if you want to go back to europe, you'll be renting instead

62209739
>>62209731
And why should I keep any digital prove to share with a pixel on a screen for whatever reason about what is in the legal sense something if I hadn't covered my tracks that would get me 25 years in a cell?

62209759
>>62209737
Right, in that scenario that's what they might push me to do. I think from 28' i will realistically leave since after that they'll try to introduce the exit tax and then it'll be truly over
>>62209739
Bro you're watching too many movies. Don't worry you and I know you're a larp

62209767
>>62209737
This is a good suggestion. The capital is being hidden far out of the reach of the bankrupt communists, a simple international bank card on the shell companies name to cash out inside the communist hell hole and for the communists the natural person looks on paper in the jurisdiction like the poorest poor fag possible.
>>62209759
What?

62209789
>>62209510
Malta 0€ Tax free.
183day Malta.
182 day in your hell country.

62209795
>>62209789
Maltas banks are still subject to European banking legislation. That's asked to be fucked

62209800
>>62209789
Yup Malta might make sense, though I read some review that for a guy he felt lonely. But what the fuck, i already have zero friends. Also Switzerland (0% crypto taxes if you're not a trader) but i don't wanna speak german and i find it too pristine for my taste

62209801
>>62209789
arent those shitty small countries insanely risky? looking at 2013 cyprus for example, the defaulted. you would have lost EVERYTHING at that time. how can you guarantee thats not going to happen again with the current state of the eu or am I missing something here?

62209810
>>62209801
You cannot that's the point. Any jurisdiction that has an automated data sharing agreement with EU faggotry should not be used as a place to hide capital from the EU. That includes Switzerland.

62209812
>>62209800
you have to move to less savoury country and open a dollar or euro account, there are tons of banks that allow you to do this even in the most shitty countries, or just wait to make a make it stack, you have to sacrifice something anon, you can't have you cake and eat it too

62209815
>>62209617
You must have been moving millions of Euro for that to happen. And to have been a bit unlucky.

62209819
>>62209812
>Make it stack
>Crapto 2026
Lmfao

62209824
>>62209815
it was something like 200k in one go. my neo broker doesnt have a support so I couldnt prepare the transaction in advance. it is what it is.

62209827
>>62209815
Bro I'm scared of even dropping 50k euros around and getting my shit analysed completely. I think anything upwards of that and the banks start asking questions

62209832
>>62209827
You should stop larping glow nigger

62209834
>>62209832
Kek, another high roller that drops 200k euros in and out on a daily basis. Get in line, there's too many of you
